title: pilosella glaucescens subsp. marginalis
intro: Pilosella glaucescens subsp. marginalis, also known as a plant within the dandelion family, is a subspecies of Pilosella glaucescens, a type of flowering plant native to certain regions of the world. Traditionally, Pilosella glaucescens subsp. marginalis has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of Pilosella glaucescens subsp. marginalis include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, Pilosella glaucescens subsp. marginalis is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
